title: Dynamic Dashboards: Explore the New Dashboard Experience

blocks.content:
Dynamic Dashboards is a new dashboarding experience in Grafana built on the Dashboard v2 schema. It introduces **tabs**, **auto-grid layouts**, **conditional visibility rules**, and an **interactive content outline** — making large, complex dashboards easier to build and navigate.

In this guide, you'll create a new dynamic dashboard and explore each of these features hands-on.
You'll create a new dashboard, which automatically uses the dynamic dashboard editor.
Open **Dashboards** from the navigation menu.
Create a new dashboard using the **New** button.

steps.description: Click **New** to open the creation menu.

blocks.content: Skip the data source wizard and go straight to the empty dashboard canvas.
blocks.tooltip: You can always connect data sources later from the panel editor.

blocks.content: You're now in the dynamic dashboard editor. Notice the right-side toolbar — this is where you'll find all the new features.
You'll open the Add pane to see how panels, layouts, and controls are added to a dynamic dashboard.
Click the **+** button in the right toolbar to open the Add pane.
blocks.tooltip: The Add pane is your starting point for building dashboard content.

blocks.content: The **Panel** area at the top lets you drag or click to add a new visualization panel to the dashboard.
blocks.tooltip: Click to add a panel, or drag it onto the canvas to place it precisely.

blocks.content: **Group into rows** lets you organize panels into collapsible row sections — similar to the classic dashboard experience.
blocks.tooltip: Rows help group related panels and reduce scrolling on large dashboards.

blocks.content: **Group into tabs** is a new dynamic dashboard feature. Tabs let you segment your dashboard horizontally — each tab can hold its own set of panels and rows.
blocks.tooltip: Tabs eliminate the need to split metrics across multiple dashboards.

blocks.content: You can also add **Variables**, **Annotation queries**, and **Links** directly from the Add pane as dashboard controls.
blocks.tooltip: Variables power cross-filtering and conditional visibility.

blocks.content: The Add pane gives you one place to build your entire dashboard structure — panels, layout groups, and controls.
You'll add a tab to the dashboard and explore the tab configuration options including layout and conditional visibility.
Click **Group into tabs** to add your first tab to the dashboard.
The tab configuration pane opens on the right. Rename this tab to something descriptive.
blocks.tooltip: Use descriptive names like 'Overview', 'Metrics', or 'Logs' to organize your dashboard.

blocks.content: Switch between **Custom** and **Auto** layout modes. Auto layout automatically arranges panels in a responsive grid that adapts to different screen sizes.
blocks.tooltip: Auto layout handles column count and sizing so panels reflow cleanly on any screen.

blocks.content: **Repeat options** lets you automatically duplicate this tab for each value of a variable. For example, repeat a tab per environment or region to create per-context views without manual duplication.
blocks.tooltip: Pair with a multi-value variable to generate one tab per selected value.

blocks.reftarget: button[data-testid='data-testid Value picker button Add rule']
blocks.content: The **Show / hide rules** section lets you conditionally show or hide this tab based on variable values. Click **+ Add rule** to create a visibility condition.
blocks.tooltip: Use this to show region-specific tabs only when that region is selected.

blocks.content: Tabs combined with repeat options and show/hide rules give you powerful conditional rendering — the dashboard adapts dynamically to what the viewer needs to see.
You'll add a panel using TestData so the dashboard has some content to work with.
Open the **Add** pane from the right toolbar.
Click the **Panel** area to add a new panel to the dashboard.
blocks.tooltip: This adds a panel to the currently active tab.

blocks.content: Click **Configure** to open the full panel editor where you can choose a data source and build a query.
blocks.tooltip: The panel editor gives you full control over data sources, queries, and visualization options.

blocks.content: Type **TestData** into the data source picker to select the built-in test data source.
blocks.tooltip: TestData generates sample data without needing an external connection.

blocks.content: Select **Grafana TestData** from the results.
Click **Back to dashboard** to return to the canvas with your new panel.
blocks.tooltip: The panel is added to your Overview tab with sample time series data.

blocks.content: Your dashboard now has a visualization. Next, you'll organize it using rows.
You'll group your panel into a collapsible row to see how rows work inside tabs.
Open the **Add** pane again.
Click **Group into rows** to wrap the existing panel in a collapsible row.
blocks.tooltip: Rows can be nested inside tabs to create a multi-level layout hierarchy.

blocks.content: Notice the content outline now shows the row nested inside the Overview tab, with your panel inside it. This is the power of dynamic dashboards — tabs contain rows, rows contain panels.
blocks.tooltip: The outline updates in real-time as you add and reorganize content.

blocks.content: Rows inside tabs give you two levels of organization — tabs for broad categories and rows for grouping related panels within each tab.
You'll open the content outline to see a tree-view of your entire dashboard structure.
Click the **Outline** button in the right toolbar to open the content outline pane.
blocks.tooltip: The outline gives you a bird's-eye view of all dashboard elements.

blocks.content: The content outline shows your dashboard as a navigable tree: **Dashboard** at the root, with Variables, Annotations, Links, and your tabs listed below. Click any item to jump directly to it.
blocks.tooltip: Essential for large dashboards — jump to any section instantly instead of scrolling.

blocks.content: As you add more panels, rows, and tabs, the outline tree grows with them — making even dashboards with hundreds of panels easy to navigate.
You'll open the dashboard options pane to see the top-level settings including the layout toggle.
Click the **Options** (gear) button in the right toolbar to open the dashboard options pane.
blocks.tooltip: Dashboard-level settings live here — title, description, layout, and variables.

blocks.content: Set the dashboard **Title** directly from this pane — no need to open a separate settings page.
blocks.tooltip: Quick inline editing without leaving the dashboard canvas.

blocks.content: The **Layout** toggle lets you switch the entire dashboard between **Rows** mode (classic) and **Tabs** mode (dynamic). This controls the top-level layout structure.
blocks.tooltip: Switching layouts reorganizes how all your panels are grouped.

blocks.content: The **Variables** section lets you add dashboard variables. Variables power template queries, cross-filtering, and the conditional show/hide rules you saw on tabs.
blocks.tooltip: Variables are the engine behind dynamic, interactive dashboards.

blocks.content: All dashboard configuration is accessible from the side pane — you can update settings without leaving the editor.
You'll open the code editor to see the new Dashboard v2 JSON schema that powers dynamic dashboards.
Click the **Code** button in the right toolbar to open the code editor.
blocks.tooltip: View and edit the raw dashboard definition — great for version control and automation.

blocks.content: Toggle between **JSON** and **YAML** formats. The underlying schema is Dashboard v2 — notice the `TabsLayout` kind and structured `elements` that replace the flat panel array of the classic schema.
blocks.tooltip: YAML can be easier to read for large dashboards.

blocks.content: You can also add panels and group them directly from the code view using **Add panel** and **Group panels**.
blocks.tooltip: Combines code editing with visual controls for a flexible workflow.

blocks.content: The v2 schema enables all dynamic features — tabs, auto-grid, conditional visibility — while remaining fully editable as code for GitOps and infrastructure-as-code workflows.
You'll discard this dashboard without saving so the next person can start fresh.
Click **Exit edit** to leave the dashboard editor without saving.
Click **Discard** to confirm you want to leave without saving. This removes the unsaved dashboard.
blocks.tooltip: The dashboard is not saved — the next person gets a clean slate.

blocks.content: Navigate back to the **Dashboards** list to start fresh.
All cleaned up and ready for the next person.

You've explored the key features of Dynamic Dashboards in Grafana:

- **Tabs** to segment dashboard content horizontally
- **Repeat options** to auto-generate tabs per variable value
- **Auto-grid layout** for responsive panel arrangement
- **Show/hide rules** for conditional rendering based on variables
- **Rows** nested inside tabs for multi-level organization
- **Content outline** for tree-based navigation
- **Inline dashboard options** for quick configuration
- **Edit as code** with the new Dashboard v2 schema

These features are in public preview and available in Grafana 12.4+. Create your own dynamic dashboards to organize complex observability data.
